stories.title: New HMRC Guidance for People Affected by Making Tax Digital Changes
stories.summary: HMRC has issued new guidance for self-employed workers and landlords as the Making Tax Digital system officially began rolling out in April for those earning more than £50,000 annually.

stories.title: Businesses Urged to Apply by End of July for Vaping Products Duty Registration
stories.summary: HMRC is urging businesses to apply for approval before the end of July ahead of the introduction of a new excise duty on vaping products from 1 October 2026.

stories.title: Asylum Seekers at Mercy of Home Office Hotel Closures Face Legal Challenge
stories.summary: The Home Office has closed 20 asylum seeker hotels following an earlier closure of 11, leaving many in precarious situations. Affected individuals are mounting a legal challenge against the closures.

stories.title: Risk of Democratic Interference Added to National Risk Register
stories.summary: The UK government has added the risk of democratic interference to the National Risk Register. The Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister also announced that the UK's Home Defence Programme will deliver the largest UK home defence exercise in decades.

stories.title: AI to Speed Up Justice Under Major Disclosure Reforms
stories.summary: The government has announced major reforms to criminal disclosure rules, with AI set to modernise a system written before smartphones existed, freeing up police officer time.

stories.title: FCDO Summons Iranian Chargé d'Affaires Over IRGC Proxy Group Attacks
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office summoned the Iranian Chargé d'Affaires in London in response to attacks carried out by Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps-linked proxy groups.

stories.title: UK Creates First Envoy Role to Support British Nationals Detained Abroad
stories.summary: Former Foreign Office Minister Alistair Burt has been appointed as the UK's first Envoy for Complex Consular Detentions, to support British nationals in the most difficult overseas detention cases.

stories.title: DWP Reappoints Brendan McCafferty as Chair of Nest
stories.summary: Brendan McCafferty has been reappointed as Chair of Nest (National Employment Savings Trust), effective from 31 January 2027, continuing in a role he has held since February 2022.

stories.title: Pension Schemes Act 2026 Evaluation Strategy Published
stories.summary: DWP has published the Evaluation Strategy for the Pension Schemes Act 2026, which received Royal Assent in April and introduced 12 policies aimed at improving Defined Contribution pension schemes.

stories.title: East Marine Plans: Fourth Three-Year Progress Report Published
stories.summary: DEFRA published its fourth three-year progress report on the East Marine Plans, covering the period from April 2023 to April 2026, assessing environmental and maritime planning outcomes.

stories.title: Record £26 Billion Investment to Transform UK Naval Bases
stories.summary: Defence Minister Luke Pollard confirmed a record £26 billion investment over ten years to modernise the Royal Navy's three main naval bases: HMNB Clyde, HMNB Devonport, and HMNB Portsmouth.

stories.title: Faslane Set for £15bn Upgrade to Increase Warfighting Capability
stories.summary: A £15bn modernisation of the Faslane (HMNB Clyde) naval base will support thousands of jobs in west Scotland and significantly boost the Royal Navy's warfighting capability.

stories.title: Families of RAF Chinook Crash Victims Have Legal Case Dismissed
stories.summary: A High Court judge has ruled that a legal challenge brought by families of RAF Chinook helicopter crash victims against the Ministry of Defence will not be allowed to proceed.

stories.title: UK Launches Call for Input on Deepening Trade Relationships with Indonesia, Philippines, UAE and Uruguay
stories.summary: The Department for Business and Trade has launched a public consultation to inform how the UK can deepen trade relationships with four key partners: Indonesia, the Philippines, the United Arab Emirates, and Uruguay.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: DSIT Annual Report and Accounts 2025–2026 Published
stories.summary: The Department for Science, Innovation and Technology has published its annual report and accounts for the year ending 31 March 2026, outlining activities, achievements, and finances.

stories.title: Research Published on Social Media Restriction Pilots for 13–17 Year Olds
stories.summary: DSIT has published qualitative research commissioned from Savanta exploring the impact of social media restrictions on 13–17 year olds in the UK, as the government considers further policy action.

stories.title: NHS England Launches New Quality Strategy for NHS-Funded Care
stories.summary: NHS England has launched a new Quality Strategy for NHS-funded care in England, setting out actions for NHS organisations and partners to improve care quality across the health system.

stories.title: Nottinghamshire ADHD Service to Pilot New Digitally Supported Pathway
stories.summary: The ADEPT-N project has been announced, with Nottinghamshire's ADHD service set to pilot a new digitally supported care pathway for patients, aiming to improve access and outcomes.

stories.title: Royal Mail Issues Delay Warning Across 216 UK Postcode Areas
stories.summary: As of 14 July, Royal Mail has warned of delivery delays affecting 216 postcode areas across the UK, including Wolverhampton, Swindon, Oxford, Carmarthen, Hull, and Bradford.

stories.title: Post Office Plus Launches as Royal Mail Delays Delivery Reforms
stories.summary: Post Office Plus has been launched as a new service, while Royal Mail's plan to end Saturday second-class deliveries has been delayed, with the nationwide rollout now scheduled for early 2026 following extended trials.
